name: Lefse
recipeInstructions:
1. Add lard, salt, and 2 cups flour to the scalded milk. Mix well over low heat.
2. Remove from heat. Sift 2 cups of flour on a board, add warm dough, and work in additional flour until smooth. Knead thoroughly and let cool. This dough can be rolled immediately or stored in a cool place for a couple of days, then stored in a plastic bag.
3. Roll the dough into small patties about hamburger size with even sealed edges.
4. Lightly flour the board and roll the patty gently using a Scandinavian rolling pin until it is almost as large as the lefse griddle.
5. Transfer the dough to the griddle, brown lightly, prick any air bubbles, turn, and brown the other side. Adjust heat from high to low if necessary.
6. Serve with butter or butter and sugar (Note: If you don't have a Scandinavian rolling pin or lefse griddle, use a regular rolling pin and griddle. This is similar to a Swedish tortilla! We typically spread butter and sprinkled sugar over the lefse, then roll them up and enjoy!).
